memory.summary: With a free afternoon and low energy, I'm leaning toward the quick win chore rather than starting the term project. It feels better to preserve my social time for Thursday.
I see that delaying the project compounds my Thursday stress, contradicting my earlier impulse to save energy for social time. I must renegotiate this boundary immediately.
Realizing the scope is too tight means I can't protect my Thursday social time without risking the project's success. I need to renegotiate the deadline with prof_assignment immediately.
Renegotiating with the cohort confirms my boundary holds; we've added buffer time, so Thursday remains safe. This aligns my project scope with our mutual social needs.
Renegotiating the timeline with cohort_3 successfully protects my Thursday social time, aligning project scope with our mutual needs.
Renegotiating with the cohort confirmed our timeline adjustment protects my Thursday social time, aligning project scope with mutual needs.
I just messaged cohort_3 to add a buffer, confirming my boundary holds. Our timeline adjustment now safely protects my Thursday social time.
I've just messaged the cohort to add a buffer, solidifying our timeline adjustment. This protects my Thursday social time and aligns project scope with our mutual needs.
Their warm thanks confirms our boundary holds; I feel lighter knowing Thursday is truly protected.

I successfully renegotiated the deadline with cohort_3, prioritizing our relationship and protecting my Thursday social time. This direct boundary-setting confirms that mutual needs outweigh rigid timelines.
The cohort's immediate confirmation that Thursday is locked in validates our boundary; I feel lighter knowing we've successfully prioritized our shared relationship over rigid timelines.
Re-locking Thursday with cohort_3 solidifies our boundary; their immediate agreement confirms that protecting our shared time is more important than the original deadline.

desires: Ensure the cohort feels I genuinely care about their well-being, not just my own schedule, by making sure this boundary doesn't make them feel like an afterthought.
Avoid falling back into old habits of sacrificing relationships for rigid deadlines before Thursday is truly over.
Protect that sense of lightness and mutual understanding we just built so it doesn't evaporate under the pressure of the term project.

goals.description: Send the follow-up text to prof_assignment explicitly locking Thursday as a non-negotiable boundary, framing it as a necessity for sustainable focus rather than an excuse.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value dictates that maintaining trust with prof_assignment is non-negotiable; if I don't secure this buffer now, they will perceive me as unreliable or incompetent. Since I already messaged cohort_3 at 15:45 about scope issues, their trust is contingent on me following through with the academic boundary. By framing this as a hard boundary on my energy and focus (deliberate) rather than an excuse, I protect our relationship while securing the time I actually need. This prevents the guilt of choosing the 'quick win' later because the high-stakes project scope is now under control.

goals.description: Execute the trivial easy chore before bed to secure a tiny, reliable win without burning out further.
goals.rationale: I cannot commit to deep work on the term project until I've successfully defended my space with both parties. Since I just messaged cohort_3, I need to ensure they feel heard before I dive back into the grind. Rushing the project now risks making me look like I'm abandoning them; waiting ensures I can engage with both parties authentically. However, given the immediate deadline of 22:00 for this chore, I am deferring it slightly to prioritize the critical negotiation first, as failing g1 would invalidate my entire strategy.

agent.last_reasoning: g1 is critical: without securing this boundary, prof_assignment will perceive me as unreliable, risking their trust and my academic standing. The deliberate approach frames this as energy management rather than avoidance, protecting our relationship while ensuring I can deliver quality work later.

goals.description: Finalize the core structure of the term project by 18:00 today to ensure I have a solid foundation before Thursday.
goals.rationale: This directly addresses my primary anxiety about looking incompetent on Thursday. Given my affiliation values, maintaining trust with prof_assignment and avoiding public failure is critical for preserving our relationship dynamic. The 'soft' hardness means this won't drain my energy reserves immediately, allowing me to protect my Thursday evening with cohort_3.

goals.description: Attend leisure_now (relaxing option) at 19:30 to decompress after work.
goals.rationale: I need to recover from the sleep debt and stress before engaging with others tomorrow. However, since I am already behind on the project, I must be careful not to let this quick win distract me; it's only valid if done strictly after the high-priority task is secured.

desires: Secure the term project's core structure by Wednesday so I don't have to scramble and look incompetent on Thursday.
Protect my Thursday evening with cohort_3 from any last-minute panic or cancellations caused by my own procrastination.
Avoid letting the 'quick win' chore feel like a betrayal of my actual priorities, since it's just an easy distraction while I'm already behind.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Term project (due in 3 days): progress 0.10 -> 0.28. (you realize) Each hour put into future_project now removes ~1.5h of crunch later and lowers Thursday stress; procrastinating compounds it.

goals.description: Immediately renegotiate the project scope and timeline with prof_assignment to secure a buffer, preventing future credibility loss.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value dictates that maintaining trust with prof_assignment is non-negotiable. Given my recent insight that procrastination compounds stress, I must address this proactively rather than letting the deadline loom. Renegotiating now protects my reputation and reduces the 'soft' hardness of the task's emotional weight.

goals.description: Finalize the core structure of the term project (targeting ~45% completion) before moving to social boundaries.
goals.rationale: With the timeline secured, I can proceed with confidence. This balances my need for growth against the risk of falling behind. It is 'soft' enough to not drain my reserves immediately but critical enough to ensure I don't appear incompetent on Thursday.

goals.description: Re-establish boundaries with cohort_3 regarding their planned social time, explicitly stating my current focus needs.
goals.rationale: While preserving closeness with cohort_3 is vital, doing so right now risks triggering unnecessary conflict or appearing unreliable if done while I am still deep in work mode. I will handle this after securing the project and completing a small win (quick_chore) to build momentum without burning out.

desires: Re-establish the boundary with my close friend group (cohort_3) to prevent them from perceiving me as unreliable, even if it means sacrificing some of their planned social time.
Avoid the compounding stress of Thursday by immediately renegotiating the project timeline or scope with the professor before I fall behind and lose credibility.

action.name: message

args.content: Hey, I've realized the current scope is too tight. To protect our relationship and avoid crunch on Thursday, can we renegotiate the deadline to give me a proper buffer? I'm committed to doing quality work, but I need realistic time.

action.reasoning: g1 is highest priority; my affiliation value demands proactive trust maintenance with prof_assignment rather than letting procrastination compound stress. This assertive boundary setting prevents future credibility loss while aligning with my long-term planning horizon.
